SEARCH

Class AssetManager

java.lang.Object
AssetManager
public class AssetManager extends Object
Downloads game assets from the ESIEE web server and stores them locally.

On each launch, checks which assets are missing from the local assets/ folder and downloads them from the remote server. Files already present are skipped. Each download is attempted up to 3 times before being marked as failed. If any downloads fail, the game continues in offline mode with whatever assets are available.

Version:
2026.04.17
Author:
Barnabe Jouanard

  • Constructor Details

    • AssetManager

      public AssetManager()
      Creates a new asset manager.

  • Method Details

    • allAssetsCached

      public boolean allAssetsCached()
      Checks whether all assets are already present in the local folder.
      Returns:
      true if every file in aAessetsList exists locally

    • countMissingAssets

      public int countMissingAssets()
      Returns the total number of assets that need to be downloaded.
      Returns:
      count of files not yet present in the local folder

    • downloadAssets

      public void downloadAssets()
      Downloads all missing assets from the assets url.

      Each file is attempted up to 3 times. If a file still fails after all retries, it is skipped and the game will run in offline mode (missing images appear as blank panels).

    • downloadFile

      private boolean downloadFile(String pFileName, File pLocalFile)
      Downloads a single file from the remote server to the local path.
      Parameters:
      pFileName - the filename on the server (e.g. "home.gif")
      pLocalFile - the local destination file
      Returns:
      true if the download succeeded; false otherwise

    • getCurrentFile

      public String getCurrentFile()
      Returns the name of the most recent file processed by downloadAssets().
      Returns:
      the current filename (may be empty)

    • getCurrentProgress

      public int getCurrentProgress()
      Returns the 1-based index of the most recent asset processed by downloadAssets().
      Returns:
      progress count in the range [0..getTotalAssets()]

    • getTotalAssets

      public int getTotalAssets()
      Returns the total number of assets configured for download.
      Returns:
      total assets count

    • isComplete

      public boolean isComplete()
      Indicates whether the asset download phase has completed.
      Returns:
      true once downloadAssets() has finished

    • getErrorMessage

      public String getErrorMessage()
      Returns a human-readable error message when at least one download failed.
      Returns:
      error message, or null when no errors occurred
